title: Reconstituting LRRK2RCKW on Microtubules for cryo-EM studies
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ribv","text":"This protocol contains a short instruction for reconstituting LRRK2RCKW on microtubules for cryo-EM studies with or without kinase inhibitors present.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":67,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

7.4","unit":28}},"7":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"80","guid":"6CACE130252C11EB82B49FFAD3BF1A58","id":0,"label":"NaCl","unit":28}},"8":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"0.5","guid":"785B7320252C11EB82B49FFAD3BF1A58","id":0,"label":"TCEP","unit":28}},"9":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"2.5","guid":"82EEF820252C11EB82B49FFAD3BF1A58","id":0,"label":"MgCl2","unit":28}}}}
steps: Add purified LRRK2 RCKW and unpolymerized bovine tubulin dimer in a 2:1 molar ratio into the polymerization buffer (2 LRRK2 RCKW s for each tubulin dimer). (Recommended total size: 10 µL ) Note: Concentration of LRRK2 RCKW has to be at least 2.5 micromolar (µM) to see filaments occur. Has been tested with multiple variants. For Snead, Matyszewski, Dickey et al, all filaments were formed at 4.5 micromolar (µM) LRRK2 RCKW and 2.25 micromolar (µM) tubulin dimer. Note: NaCl concentration at this step should remain at around 90 millimolar (mM) or less, often imposing a limit on LRRK2 RCKW concentration allowed to be used. Higher salt concentration will prevent or reduce filament formation.
Allow the mixture to polymerize at Room temperature for at least 01:00:00 .
Prepare cryo-EM grids. Recommended grids to use: Lacey Carbon on copper, 300 mesh, made by EMS. Glow discharged right before plunge freezing for 00:00:45 at 20 mA current.

Dilute the sample 3-fold in the LRRK2 reaction buffer. (Recommended mixture: 4 µL, sample + 8 µL, LRRK2 buffer ). This step reduces glycerol to be within acceptable levels for cryo-EM. This will reduce the effective concentration of components, but the dilution of LRRK2 RCKW and tubulin might be non-linear due to filaments bundling to each other. The minimum concentration mentioned in Step 1 only applies to incubation step.

Apply diluted sample to grid and plunge freeze using your usual Vitrobot settings. (Ex. 4 µL, sample , blotted for 00:00:04 at blot force 20 for our particular Vitrobot (FEI); conditions might vary from one machine to another).

If incubating with LRRK2 kinase inhibitors, add those before adding tubulin to LRRK2 RCKW and allow to incubate for at least 00:05:00 . In Snead, Matyszewski, Dickey et al, MLi-2 was added at 5 micromolar (µM), (final concentration after adding tubulin) .
creator: Mariusz Matyszewski
